items.q: Is CAD the same as CAM?
items.correct: No — CAD is designing and modelling a product on computer; CAM uses computer-controlled machines to manufacture it. CAD data is often exported to drive CAM machines.
items.misconception: CAD software physically manufactures the product.
items.random_wrong: CAM is a program used for editing photographs of products.
items.why: Students blur design and manufacture because the acronyms are similar; the design-data-to-machine workflow is the key relationship to understand.

items.q: Is 3D printing additive or subtractive manufacture?
items.correct: Additive — material is built up layer by layer from a digital model. Milling and laser cutting are subtractive because they remove material from stock.
items.misconception: 3D printing carves the shape out of a solid block of material.
items.why: Students group all CNC processes together; the additive/subtractive distinction explains 3D printing's low waste and geometric freedom.

items.q: Does a CNC machine design the product itself?
items.correct: No — a CNC machine only follows programmed toolpaths generated by a human via CAD/CAM software; it executes instructions, it does not make design decisions.
items.misconception: CNC machines automatically decide the best design and make it.
items.random_wrong: CNC machines are steered manually with a remote control.
items.why: Students overestimate machine autonomy; understanding that humans create the program clarifies where skill and responsibility sit in digital manufacture.
It's Computer-aided manufacture. “Computer-assisted modelling” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which of these is an additive manufacturing process?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 3D printing. “Laser cutting” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which is a key advantage of CAD over manual drawing?
items.correct: Designs can be edited and tested virtually before production
items.misconception: It removes the need for any design skill
items.random_wrong: It guarantees the product will sell
items.why: Correct answer: Designs can be edited and tested virtually before production. “It removes the need for any design skill” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How is a CNC machine controlled?
items.correct: By programmed coordinate instructions such as G-code
items.misconception: By a hand-operated joystick during every cut
items.random_wrong: By voice commands from the operator
items.why: Correct answer: By programmed coordinate instructions such as G-code. “By a hand-operated joystick during every cut” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: An FDM 3D printer deposits layers 0.2 mm thick. How many layers are needed for a part 30 mm tall?
items.why: Exact answer: 150 layers. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
